pattern: A two-dimensional dynamic program over prefix length and number of pieces, using prefix XOR for any segment's value, minimizes the worst segment cost.
hints: The XOR of any contiguous block is the XOR of two prefix values, so block costs are O(1) to query.
Think of placing cuts: the best way to split the first i elements into j pieces depends on where the last piece begins.
Let dp[i][j] be the minimum possible maximum-XOR for the first i elements in j subarrays; transition over the start of the final subarray, combining its XOR with the recorded best for the prefix.

edge_inputs.description: three pieces with a zero-xor middle
k equals length, every element alone
single piece is the whole-array xor
must isolate a large element into its own block

stress.description: moderate arrays with a random valid k - keeps the brute oracle tractable
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, 16)
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 64) for _ in range(n)]
    k = rng.randint(1, n)
    return [nums, k]
stress.note: n kept small because the oracle enumerates cut placements
